visual:
NEW data context: "Team Scores" (HORIZONTAL orientation)
  - Data Table: Red Team: 25, Green Team: 55, Yellow Team: 70, Blue Team: 40
  - Scale Preview System available
guide: "Last one! Here are scores from four teams. Yellow Team won with 70 points."
guide_2: "You've done this vertical and horizontal now. Same process every time: check the numbers, choose the scale."
prompt: "Which scale works best for this data?"
student_action: [Select: Scale of 1 / Scale of 2 / Scale of 5 / Scale of 10]
correct_answer: Scale of 10 (most efficient; all values end in 0 or 5; range up to 70)
on_correct: "Good choice. All values end in 0 or 5, and the biggest is 70. Scale of 10 gives you a clean graph."
on_incorrect: (Scale of 1 or 2) "That would need a lot of lines on the axis. All the values end in 0 or 5—which bigger scale would be easier to read?"
on_incorrect_2: (Scale of 5) "That works! But notice the biggest value is 70. Scale of 10 would give you fewer lines and still show everything exactly."
visual_2: Bar Graph creation tool (HORIZONTAL) with Scale of 10 active. Empty graph with axis 0-80 showing tick marks at 0, 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60, 70, 80.
guide_3: "Now create the graph. Place each bar at the right length."
student_action_2: Set bar lengths for all 4 categories (click-to-set)
prompt_2: "Click to place the bar for Red Team (25 points)."
student_action_3: Click-to-set: Student clicks at height 25 (between 20 and 30 tick marks)
on_correct_2: Bar locks at 25. "Right at 25—halfway between 20 and 30."
on_incorrect_3: "25 is halfway between 20 and 30. Find that spot."
prompt_3: "Click to place the bar for Blue Team (40 points)."
prompt_4: "Click to place the bar for Green Team (55 points)."
prompt_5: "Click to place the bar for Yellow Team (70 points)."
on_complete: "That's it. You chose the scale and placed every bar exactly where it should be, some exactly on the lines and some halfway between."
